steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: “Such interpretations are ultimately speculative” and “the object's gray coloration may be an incidental effect of radiation, solar wind, or collisions with other objects rather than evidence of its physical makeup.” Though must connect these clauses in a concessive (contrasting) relationship.

steps.title: Apply the rule for two independent clauses with 'though'
steps.content:
When though marks a concessive relationship between two independent clauses, Standard English requires the pattern IC, though; IC—comma before though, semicolon after. This correctly separates the two independent statements while marking their concessive relationship. Options A and B fail to place a semicolon after though (A leaves no punctuation; B adds an incorrect comma), and Option C incorrectly places the semicolon before though rather than after.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
